speaker: NORFOLK
text: Then, that in all you writ to Rome, or else

speaker: NORFOLK
text: To foreign princes, “ego et rex meus”

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Was still inscribed, in which you brought the King

speaker: NORFOLK
text: To be your servant.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Then, that without the knowledge

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Either of king or council, when you went

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Ambassador to the Emperor, you made bold

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To carry into Flanders the great seal.

speaker: SURREY
text: Item, you sent a large commission

speaker: SURREY
text: To Gregory de Cassado, to conclude,

speaker: SURREY
text: Without the King’s will or the state’s allowance,

speaker: SURREY
text: A league between his Highness and Ferrara.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That out of mere ambition you have caused

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Your holy hat to be stamped on the King’s coin.

speaker: SURREY
text: Then, that you have sent innumerable substance—

speaker: SURREY
text: By what means got I leave to your own conscience—

speaker: SURREY
text: To furnish Rome and to prepare the ways

speaker: SURREY
text: You have for dignities, to the mere undoing

speaker: SURREY
text: Of all the kingdom. Many more there are

speaker: SURREY
text: Which, since they are of you, and odious,

speaker: SURREY
text: I will not taint my mouth with.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: O, my lord,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Press not a falling man too far! ’Tis virtue.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: His faults lie open to the laws; let them,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Not you, correct him. My heart weeps to see him

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: So little of his great self.

speaker: SURREY
text: I forgive him.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Lord Cardinal, the King’s further pleasure is—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Because all those things you have done of late

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: By your power legative within this kingdom

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Fall into th’ compass of a praemunire—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That therefore such a writ be sued against you,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To forfeit all your goods, lands, tenements,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Chattels, and whatsoever, and to be

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Out of the King’s protection. This is my charge.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And so we’ll leave you to your meditations

speaker: NORFOLK
text: How to live better. For your stubborn answer

speaker: NORFOLK
text: About the giving back the great seal to us,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The King shall know it and, no doubt, shall thank you.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: So, fare you well, my little good Lord Cardinal.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: So, farewell to the little good you bear me.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Farewell? A long farewell to all my greatness!

speaker: WOLSEY
text: This is the state of man: today he puts forth

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The tender leaves of hopes; tomorrow blossoms

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And bears his blushing honors thick upon him;

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The third day comes a frost, a killing frost,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And when he thinks, good easy man, full surely

speaker: WOLSEY
text: His greatness is a-ripening, nips his root,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And then he falls, as I do. I have ventured,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Like little wanton boys that swim on bladders,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: This many summers in a sea of glory,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: But far beyond my depth. My high-blown pride

speaker: WOLSEY
text: At length broke under me and now has left me,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Weary and old with service, to the mercy

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Of a rude stream that must forever hide me.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Vain pomp and glory of this world, I hate you.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I feel my heart new opened. O, how wretched

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Is that poor man that hangs on princes’ favors!

speaker: WOLSEY
text: There is betwixt that smile we would aspire to,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That sweet aspect of princes, and their ruin,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: More pangs and fears than wars or women have;

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And when he falls, he falls like Lucifer,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Never to hope again.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Why, how now, Cromwell?

speaker: CROMWELL
text: I have no power to speak, sir.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: What, amazed

speaker: WOLSEY
text: At my misfortunes? Can thy spirit wonder

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A great man should decline? Nay, an you weep,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I am fall’n indeed.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: How does your Grace?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Why, well.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Never so truly happy, my good Cromwell.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I know myself now, and I feel within me

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A peace above all earthly dignities,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A still and quiet conscience. The King has cured me—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I humbly thank his Grace—and from these shoulders,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: These ruined pillars, out of pity, taken

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A load would sink a navy: too much honor.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: O, ’tis a burden, Cromwell, ’tis a burden

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Too heavy for a man that hopes for heaven.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: I am glad your Grace has made that right use of it.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I hope I have. I am able now, methinks,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Out of a fortitude of soul I feel,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: To endure more miseries and greater far

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Than my weak-hearted enemies dare offer.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: What news abroad?

speaker: CROMWELL
text: The heaviest and the worst

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Is your displeasure with the King.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: God bless him.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: The next is that Sir Thomas More is chosen

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Lord Chancellor in your place.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That’s somewhat sudden.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: But he’s a learnèd man. May he continue

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Long in his Highness’ favor and do justice

speaker: WOLSEY
text: For truth’s sake and his conscience, that his bones,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: When he has run his course and sleeps in blessings,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: May have a tomb of orphans’ tears wept on him.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: What more?

speaker: CROMWELL
text: That Cranmer is returned with welcome,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Installed Lord Archbishop of Canterbury.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That’s news indeed.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Last, that the Lady Anne,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Whom the King hath in secrecy long married,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: This day was viewed in open as his queen,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Going to chapel, and the voice is now

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Only about her coronation.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: There was the weight that pulled me down. O Cromwell,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The King has gone beyond me. All my glories

speaker: WOLSEY
text: In that one woman I have lost forever.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: No sun shall ever usher forth mine honors,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Or gild again the noble troops that waited

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Upon my smiles. Go, get thee from me, Cromwell.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I am a poor fall’n man, unworthy now

speaker: WOLSEY
text: To be thy lord and master. Seek the King;

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That sun, I pray, may never set! I have told him

speaker: WOLSEY
text: What and how true thou art. He will advance thee;

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Some little memory of me will stir him—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I know his noble nature—not to let

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Thy hopeful service perish too. Good Cromwell,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Neglect him not. Make use now, and provide

speaker: WOLSEY
text: For thine own future safety.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: O, my lord,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Must I then leave you? Must I needs forgo

speaker: CROMWELL
text: So good, so noble, and so true a master?

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Bear witness, all that have not hearts of iron,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: With what a sorrow Cromwell leaves his lord.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: The King shall have my service, but my prayers

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Forever and forever shall be yours.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Cromwell, I did not think to shed a tear

speaker: WOLSEY
text: In all my miseries, but thou hast forced me,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Out of thy honest truth, to play the woman.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Let’s dry our eyes. And thus far hear me, Cromwell,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And when I am forgotten, as I shall be,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And sleep in dull cold marble, where no mention

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Of me more must be heard of, say I taught thee;

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Say Wolsey, that once trod the ways of glory

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And sounded all the depths and shoals of honor,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Found thee a way, out of his wrack, to rise in,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A sure and safe one, though thy master missed it.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Mark but my fall and that that ruined me.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Cromwell, I charge thee, fling away ambition!

speaker: WOLSEY
text: By that sin fell the angels; how can man, then,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The image of his maker, hope to win by it?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Love thyself last; cherish those hearts that hate thee.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Corruption wins not more than honesty.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Still in thy right hand carry gentle peace

speaker: WOLSEY
text: To silence envious tongues. Be just, and fear not.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Let all the ends thou aim’st at be thy country’s,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Thy God’s, and truth’s. Then if thou fall’st, O Cromwell,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Thou fall’st a blessèd martyr.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Serve the King. And, prithee, lead me in.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: There take an inventory of all I have

speaker: WOLSEY
text: To the last penny; ’tis the King’s. My robe

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And my integrity to heaven is all

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I dare now call mine own. O Cromwell, Cromwell,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Had I but served my God with half the zeal

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I served my king, He would not in mine age

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Have left me naked to mine enemies.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Good sir, have patience.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: So I have. Farewell,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The hopes of court! My hopes in heaven do dwell.
